text.title: CI Tools, Services, and Best Practices
text.summary: CLI tools, agent-friendly services, limitations, and operational best practices for a production CI pipeline.

list.item: firecrawl / jina reader: LLM-ready page extraction with JS rendering — primary fallback for SPA pages
playwright: Custom JS-rendered scrape when API and jina both fail
rss-parser (npm) / feedparser (py): Newsroom + blog ingestion — use RSS before scraping
visualping: Diff alerts on rendered pages — cheap raw signal, no synthesis
phantombuster: LinkedIn hiring/post extraction — use with proxy rotation and respect ToS
apify actors: Headless scrape for G2, LinkedIn, Product Hunt — pay-per-run
crunchbase API: Funding/M&A/leadership — ground truth for firmographic data
ahrefs / semrush API: SEO/SEM moves, top pages, keyword shifts
g2 Buyer Intent API: Review velocity and intent signals

rule.statement: Emit one event per delta, never aggregate inside the collector — keeps the audit trail clean and lets the synthesizer see the raw timeline.
rule.rationale: Aggregated events hide the sequence of competitor moves. The synthesizer needs the timeline to detect acceleration patterns.

rule.statement: Separate "what changed" (Haiku, mechanical) from "so what" (Opus, judgment). Mixing them costs 10x and adds hallucination.
rule.rationale: Opus reasoning on raw HTML diffs is wasteful and noisy. Haiku distills the signal; Opus interprets the pattern.

rule.statement: Subscribe to competitor changelog/RSS first; scraping is the fallback. APIs > RSS > scrape > screenshot diff.
rule.rationale: APIs are reliable and ToS-compliant. Scraping is fragile and legally ambiguous. Use the most reliable source available per competitor.

rule.statement: Track yourself with the same pipeline to catch what your own marketing site exposes to competitors.
rule.rationale: CI is bidirectional. What you publish about pricing, hiring, and product reveals your strategy to the same tools your competitors use.

rule.statement: Calibrate threat scores against actual lost deals quarterly; recalibrate the prompt if the score does not predict losses.
rule.rationale: An uncalibrated threat score becomes a noise source. Quarterly calibration ties CI to revenue impact.

antipattern.description: LLM synthesis when the scraped page is paywalled or JS-rendered and returns an empty body.
antipattern.reason: Agents invent competitor features from training data when the source page is empty. Always require source citation per claim; reject unsourced assertions.

antipattern.description: Daily monitors on pricing pages hidden behind "Contact Sales" generating false silence.
antipattern.reason: These pages never change for anonymous crawlers. Cross-check G2/Capterra and customer reviews instead for pricing signal.

antipattern.description: Trusting win-rate improvement claims (e.g., 16% to 45%) from vendor case studies without auditing methodology.
antipattern.reason: Win-rate improvements conflate CI rollout with simultaneous sales enablement and pricing changes. Survivorship bias is severe.
